reasoning: Fidelity composite critically low: 0.40 (3/3 dimensions evaluated). Document quality below minimum threshold for publication.

concerns.analysis: The output JSON is missing significant portions of the document, including entire pages and sections. For example, pages 4 through 12, which cover NCDIT's role, language access efforts, and the first part of the Needs Assessment, are completely absent. This is explicitly indicated by multiple '_omitted' placeholders in the JSON structure.
concerns.output_quote: The JSON skips elements between reading_order 21 and 127, and contains various other large gaps marked as '_omitted'.
concerns.location: Throughout the document, various sections and pages.

concerns.analysis: The action item table for Factor 2 on page 16 of the source PDF contains 11 data rows. The corresponding table in the output JSON (element index reading_order 157) contains only the header row, with all 11 data rows missing. This represents a 100% loss of data rows for this table.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The 11 data rows in the action items table under 'Five-Year Action Plan' for Factor 2 on page 16.
concerns.output_quote: The table element at reading_order 157 contains only the 'Phase' and 'Action Items' header cells and no data rows.
concerns.location: Factor 2 Five-Year Action Plan, page 16, reading_order 157.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: In Section 1 (page 3), the source PDF lists four distinct parts addressed in implementation sections (Sections 2 to 7). The output JSON (reading_order 19) only captures the first two items ('Overview of Standard' and 'Current NCDIT Systems and Practices'), completely omitting items 3 ('Implementation Goals') and 4 ('5-Year Action Plan').
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
3. Implementation Goals: provides an overview of the overarching goals NCDIT will pursue to meet compliance objectives
4. 5-Year Action Plan: a list of detailed time-bound action items...
concerns.output_quote: The list in element 19 ends after the description of 'Current NCDIT Systems and Practices'.
concerns.location: SECTION 1: INTRODUCTION AND BACKGROUND, page 3, reading_order 19.

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy is inconsistent. Major sections (Section 1, Section 2, etc.) are correctly rendered as H2. Subsections (1.1, 2.1, etc.) are rendered as H3 in sections 1, 2, 3, 5, and 6. However, in Section 4 (reading_order 41) and Section 7 (reading_order 48), the subsections (4.1 and 7.1) are rendered as H2, which is the same level as the parent section, breaking the nesting logic used elsewhere in the document and intended by the source's structure.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: 4.1 NOTIFICATION OF LANGUAGE ASSISTANCE SERVICES PLAN (Page 41); 7.1 Monitoring, Evaluation, and Reporting Plan (Page 48)
concerns.output_quote: {"type": "heading", "level": 2, "text": "4.1 NOTIFICATION OF LANGUAGE ASSISTANCE SERVICES PLAN"} (reading_order 41); {"type": "heading", "level": 2, "text": "7.1 Monitoring, Evaluation, and Reporting Plan"} (reading_order 48)

concerns.analysis: There are two distinct structural corruption issues in the tables. 1) Misidentified headers: For tables spanning multiple pages (e.g., action items on pages 14 and 19), the pipeline creates a new table object for the continuation and incorrectly marks the first data row as a header row ('is_header_row': true). 2) Complex table corruption: The Appendix 1 table on page 54 is structurally corrupted. The cell 'Council of Government Regions (COGRs)' in the header row lacks the required colspan (it sh
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Table rows starting with 'I' on page 14 and 'II' on page 19; Header 'Council of Government Regions (COGRs)' on page 54 spanning columns A through R.
concerns.output_quote: Index 140 (reading_order 14): 'is_header_row': true for data row 'I...'; Index 186 (reading_order 19): 'is_header_row': true for data row 'II...'; Index 544 (reading_order 54): Header 'Council of Government Regions (COGRs)' has 'colspan': 1.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: On page 29, the output JSON fails to correctly parse the full title of a referenced document, leaving a gap where 'Interpretation Waiver Form Guidance' should be. Additionally, on page 54, a URL found in the source text is omitted from the corresponding paragraph in the output.
concerns.location: Language Access Services, In-Person Interpretation, page 29, reading_order 297 and 299.

concerns.analysis: The output fails to preserve a specific hyperlink from the source. On page 29, the link text 'Interpretation Waiver Form Guidance' is completely missing from the output text block, creating a content gap where the link should be.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: ...set in the Proposed Language Access Policy and Interpretation Waiver Form Guidance document.
concerns.output_quote: Index 297: '...Proposed Language Access Policy and '; Index 299: ' document.'

action_items: Fidelity composite critically low: 0.40 (3/3 dimensions evaluated). Document quality below minimum threshold for publication.
